improvements_noted: Target r/algotrading next run — technically sophisticated audience who will value AI aggregation angle
Try searching for 'contradictory trading advice' and 'analysis paralysis' as pain keywords — more specific than 'information overload'
Consider timing posts to Tuesday/Wednesday when Reddit trading threads are most active

reply_style_notes: r/Daytrading responds well to personal anecdote + practical framework — avoid being too salesy
r/investing audience is more conservative — lead with risk management angle, not returns

subreddit_notes.Daytrading: Active community, high tolerance for trading tool mentions if framed as 'what works for me'
subreddit_notes.stocks: Skeptical of anything that sounds like promotion — be extra value-first here
subreddit_notes.investing: Long-term mindset — frame CWT as saving research time, not as a signal service
subreddit_notes.Forex: Technically sophisticated — can mention consensus mechanics without over-explaining
subreddit_notes.StockMarket: Mixed skill level — good for beginner-friendly explanations

competitor_intel.Collective2: Pivoting toward AI strategy generation — worth monitoring for feature overlap
competitor_intel.SentimentTrader: Primarily institutional — not a direct competitor for retail audience
competitor_intel.The Motley Fool: Declining brand trust among younger traders — opportunity to position as the modern alternative
